title: Bluegrass Farm Kit from Studio Calico!
excerpt: You all - this may be one of my favorite kits ever. And though I didn't have much time to work on these projects - about 3 hours as soon as I got back from a trip to the east…
body:
You all - this may be one of my favorite kits ever. And though I didn't have much time to work on these projects - about 3 hours as soon as I got back from a trip to the east coast, to be completely honest about it - I LOVED all the custom elements in Bluegrass Farm. There are plenty of supplies leftover, which I have already used for an assignment for STITCHED, a class I am contributing to with Paige Evans. Though I consider myself to be a pretty fast scrapbooker, when I am surrounded by supplies that I love, I want to take my time to absorb it and really think of how to best use everything. Without that luxury this time around, I took my favorite bits of Bluegrass Farm and roller with it. I used my Epson XP-950 Wireless Photo Printer to print all of these photos. It was easy to turn the selfie of the three of us into B&W using the PicTapGo app. Then I simply selected the photos I wanted to print and wirelessly sent them to my printer. Easy peasy. Using the Project Life main kit only on the left side and various add-on's on the right side, I created a spread that highlighted our recent trip to Florida. Also taking into account the fact that Jack's a great traveler who is 6 months old and experiencing many firsts - like his first wedding! I was assigned PL on one side and a layout on the other. These often appear this way in my albums, but I love the intentional look of this one where the colors feel completely balanced.

Now for the Bluegrass Farm Scrapbook kits. Using only the main kit, I created 2 layouts, this one used a 12x12 photo printed by Persnickety Prints. Just by adding a few bits into the negative space of the photo, it gave the right amount of dimension and detail. I hand cut the title from the patterned paper using the Honey Bee scissors. They are my go-to tool for fussy cutting and dealing with smaller pieces.

Add a personal touch to your paper crafting projects with STITCHED. Studio Calico Creative Team Member Paige Evans and a stellar line up of contributors will be sharing 24 unique ideas for adding hand and machine stitching to your layouts, cards and Project Life® spreads during this month long online class. We'll cover everything from different stitch types to quilting paper to adding dimension with stitching with ideas tailor made for your paper crafting projects.

Registration for this class will close on 3/31/2014 at 11:59pm EST. Class begins on April 1, 2014 and a new lesson will be posted daily through May 2nd.

Keep in mind that subscribers can shop the pre-sale each month at noon EST on the 27th. Others need to wait until the clock strikes midnight tonight. Become a subscriber and get in on all the benefits and rewards that Studio Calico has to offer!
